sourceMovie.title: Saw 3D
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Saw 3D works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saw
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Saw 3D clicked for you, Saw (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Saw 3D clicked for you, Saw III (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Saw 3D?
faq.answer: Try Saw, Saw III, Bird Box—each shares DNA with Saw 3D while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Saw 3D?
faq.answer: Saw 3D (2010) is best described as Horror, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Saw 3D part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Saw Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
